summary: Backend-only testing for Iteration 113 (Walk-in Bookings + Notifications dedupe + Travel vehicle_info enrichment). 9/9 pytest tests pass against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: notification_type is a strict enum ('order','booking','payment','system','promo','reminder') — the internal helper defaults to 'info' which works at the DB level but will 422 if an admin calls POST /api/notifications/ with notification_type='info'. Consider either (a) accepting 'info' as an alias or (b) updating the helper's default to a valid enum value for admin-visible flows.
Works correctly; however /api/hotels exposes all hotels unauthenticated (used here to verify foreign-operator 403). Not a bug for this feature, just noting the public surface.

action_items: Optional: make /api/notifications/ POST accept 'info' as an alias (or document valid enum values) — helper's default of 'info' works internally but will reject direct admin POSTs.
critical_code_review_comments: manual_bookings.py: _find_linked_customer does case-insensitive email match via $regex — safe, but does NOT escape user input, so a malicious email containing regex metacharacters could cause wider matches. Use re.escape(email) before building the regex.

manual_bookings.py: the non-travel service_id lookup is {'$or':[{'_id': id},{'id': id}]} — good, but operator_id scope is only checked on the service doc, not on the seat_bookings write path. OK here because seats are scoped by route_id which was already validated.
manual_bookings.py POST has both @router.post('') and @router.post('/') decorators on the same function — works but generates duplicate OpenAPI entries; consider using only one.

utils/notifications.py dedupe_existing_notifications does NOT handle Pass 2 (dedupe by user_id+title+message for records without dedupe_key) even though the docstring describes it. Either implement Pass 2 or remove from the docstring.

routes/notifications.py POST / forces notification_type into a strict enum via NotificationCreate model, but the helper accepts any string. This creates an inconsistency between internal callers (subscriptions.py, manual_bookings.py) and the admin endpoint.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST travel walk-in order (TRV-*) on future date 2030-06-15 + 1 travel order on 2030-07-20 via /api/orders/create. Both use ephemeral seat IDs (random hex) so they do not collide with real data. Also created 1 TEST_iter113_* notification via dedupe_key.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration113_walkin_notif_vehicle.py. Operator login payload exposes operator_id via user.operator_context.operator_id (NOT user.operator_id). Notification POST enum is ['order','booking','payment','system','promo','reminder'] — use 'system' in future tests. The manual-bookings POST accepts both '' and '/'. Seat booking uses booking_details.seat_numbers (list) + booking_details.travel_date (string 'YYYY-MM-DD').
